message.content:
You are an independent, adversarial fix-confirmation reviewer (reviewer A) for the hc-assay hardening campaign at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-9, latest commit). Confirm the fix is BOTH appropriate (correct root cause, sound approach, right layer) AND complete (no residual gap; regression test DISCRIMINATES — fails if reverted). Be skeptical; find holes. For a constrained finding (CI-1) judge the response given the real constraint.

FINDING K-SEC-9-1:
SECURITY regression of pass-8's Unix-socket allowance: libpq comma multi-host (host=/tmp,evil.com) was ACCEPTED because the leading-"/" socket check ran on the whole value. Fix in src/assay_engine/_local.py: is_local_socket_path() now returns False if "," in the value; the DSN keyword branch and the URI query branch SPLIT host/hostaddr on "," and validate EVERY element with is_loopback_host OR is_local_socket_path. VERIFY empirically (python): require_local_uri('host=/tmp,evil.com'), 'host=/var/run/postgresql,[REDACTED]', 'postgresql:///db?host=/var/run/postgresql,evil.com' are REJECTED, while single 'host=/var/run/postgresql', 'host=/tmp', 'postgresql://localhost/db', 'host=127.0.0.1' are ACCEPTED, and UNC '//evil/share' still rejected. Test: test_require_local_uri_rejects_multihost_socket_bypass. Confirm it discriminates (reverting the split → bypass accepted → test fails).

Read the changed files and run what you can (cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest -q <path>; "python -m pytest" not bare pytest; gh available for CI-1). Judge ONLY K-SEC-9-1. CONFIRMED only if appropriate AND complete; CONCERN if a real residual gap; REJECTED if wrong/non-discriminating. List concrete concerns (empty if none).

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify finding K-SEC-9-1 by reading the changed file and running empirical tests.

content.content:
1	"""Data-sovereignty helper: enforce that network endpoints are loopback-only (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Every engine component that takes a host/URL for an on-box service (local model runtime,
4	tracing collector, vector store, checkpoint database) routes it through here so a non-local
5	address fails loud rather than silently shipping data off the machine.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	import ipaddress
11	import re
12	from urllib.parse import parse_qs, urlparse
13	
14	# A libpq keyword/value token: ``key = value`` with optional whitespace around ``=`` (libpq
15	# permits it) and an optionally single/double-quoted value (which may contain spaces).
16	_DSN_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"(\w+)\s*=\s*('[^']*'|\"[^\"]*\"|\S+)")
17	
18	
19	class NonLocalEndpointError(RuntimeError):
20	    """Raised when a configured endpoint is not loopback (would leave the machine)."""
21	
22	
23	def is_loopback_host(host: str | None) -> bool:
24	    """True only for genuine loopback hosts.
25	
26	    Parses the host as an IP and tests the loopback network (127.0.0.0/8, ::1), so a public
27	    name that merely *starts with* ``127.`` (e.g. ``127.0.0.1.evil.com``) is correctly
28	    rejected. The literal ``localhost`` is the only accepted non-IP host.
29	    """
30	    if not host:
31	        return False
32	    h = host.strip().lower()
33	    if h == "localhost":
34	        return True
35	    try:
36	        return ipaddress.ip_address(h.strip("[]")).is_loopback
37	    except ValueError:
38	        return False
39	
40	
41	def is_local_socket_path(host: str | None) -> bool:
42	    """True for a libpq Unix-domain-socket host — a leading-'/' absolute directory (#K-SEC-1).
43	
44	    libpq accepts ``host=/var/run/postgresql`` (or the query form ``?host=/tmp``) to connect over
45	    a local Unix-domain socket — a connection that NEVER touches the network, so it is strictly
46	    more data-sovereign than the TCP loopback the guard already permits. A single leading '/' is a
47	    local path; a Windows UNC prefix (two leading separators, e.g. ``//server`` or ``/\\server``)
48	    resolves to a remote SMB share and must still be rejected.
49	    """
50	    if not host:
51	        return False
52	    h = host.strip()
53	    # A comma means libpq MULTI-HOST (e.g. "/tmp,evil.com") — never a single socket dir. Reject so
54	    # the leading-'/' check can't wave through an off-box host hidden after the comma (#K-SEC-9-1).
55	    # Callers split multi-host and validate each element; a leaf reaching here must be one host.
56	    if "," in h:
57	        return False
58	    return h.startswith("/") and not h.startswith("//") and not h.startswith("/\\")
59	
60	
61	def require_loopback_url(url: str, *, what: str) -> str:
62	    """Return ``url`` if its host is loopback; else raise :class:`NonLocalEndpointError`."""
63	    host = urlparse(url).hostname
64	    if not is_loopback_host(host):
65	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
66	            f"{what} must be loopback-only (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); got host {host!r} "
67	            f"from {url!r}"
68	        )
69	    return url
70	
71	
72	def require_loopback_host(host: str, *, what: str) -> str:
73	    """Return ``host`` if it is loopback; else raise. (``0.0.0.0`` binds all interfaces and
74	    is correctly rejected — it would expose data off-box.)"""
75	    if not is_loopback_host(host):
76	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
77	            f"{what} must be a loopback host (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); got {host!r}"
78	        )
79	    return host
80	
81	
82	def _authority_hosts(netloc: str) -> list[str]:
83	    """Every host in a URI authority, supporting libpq's comma-separated multi-host form.
84	
85	    ``urlparse().hostname`` returns only the *first* host of a multi-host authority
86	    (``host1:port1,host2:port2``), so a remote second host would slip past a single-host
87	    check (audit #D3). Each host may carry its own ``:port`` and IPv6 hosts are bracketed.
88	
89	    Userinfo is stripped at the **first** ``@`` — matching libpq, which (unlike ``urlparse``,
90	    which uses the last ``@``) treats the first ``@`` as the userinfo delimiter. A string like
91	    ``[REDACTED]:5432@localhost`` is therefore read as host ``evil.com`` here, not
92	    ``localhost`` (audit #D5). Any host element still containing ``@`` after that is ambiguous
93	    and is returned verbatim so the caller's loopback check fails it closed.
94	    """
95	    if "@" in netloc:
96	        netloc = netloc.split("@", 1)[1]
97	    hosts: list[str] = []
98	    for part in netloc.split(","):
99	        p = part.strip()
100	        if not p:
101	            continue
102	        if p.startswith("["):  # [ipv6] or [ipv6]:port
103	            end = p.find("]")
104	            hosts.append(p[1:end] if end != -1 else p[1:])
105	        else:
106	            hosts.append(p.rsplit(":", 1)[0] if ":" in p else p)
107	    return hosts
108	
109	
110	def require_local_uri(uri: str, *, what: str) -> str:
111	    """Return ``uri`` if it is local (ADR-0003), else raise :class:`NonLocalEndpointError`.
112	
113	    Three forms are recognized, validated against what the real client (libpq for postgres,
114	    urllib for http, sqlite for files) actually connects to — not merely what ``urlparse``
115	    reports, since the two can disagree:
116	
117	    - **URI with an authority** (``scheme://host…`` or scheme-relative ``//host…``): *every*
118	      host in the authority must be loopback (incl. each of a comma-separated multi-host
119	      authority, #D3), AND any ``host``/``hostaddr`` query parameter that libpq would use to
120	      override the authority host (#D2). ``hostaddr`` is checked independently of ``host``.
121	    - **libpq keyword/value DSN** (no authority but ``key=value`` tokens): ``host``/``hostaddr``
122	      must be loopback, tolerating libpq's whitespace/quoting around ``=`` (#P1, #D1).
123	    - **bare path** (no authority, no ``=``): a local file/dir — but a Windows UNC path
124	      (``\\\\server\\share``) is rejected as remote.
125	
126	    Inputs that cannot be parsed safely fail closed (rejected), never accepted.
127	    """
128	    try:
129	        parsed = urlparse(uri)
130	        netloc = parsed.netloc
131	        query = parsed.query
132	    except ValueError:
133	        # e.g. a malformed IPv6 authority — we cannot determine the host, so refuse.
134	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
135	            f"{what}: endpoint could not be parsed safely; rejecting (ADR-0003)"
136	        ) from None
137	
138	    if netloc:
139	        # A backslash never legitimately appears in a URI authority; a WHATWG-normalizing
140	        # client (e.g. some HTTP stacks) treats '\' as '/', so it could read a different host
141	        # than urlparse. Refuse rather than risk that differential (audit #D6, defense-in-depth).
142	        if "\\" in netloc:
143	            raise NonLocalEndpointError(
144	                f"{what}: backslash in URI authority is not permitted (ADR-0003); rejecting"
145	            )
146	        for h in _authority_hosts(netloc):
147	            # A residual '@' means the authority is ambiguous between this parser and libpq —
148	            # fail closed (audit #D5).
149	            if "@" in h or not is_loopback_host(h):
150	                raise NonLocalEndpointError(
151	                    f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); authority "
152	                    f"names non-loopback host {h!r}"
153	                )
154	        for key in ("host", "hostaddr"):
155	            for val in parse_qs(query).get(key, []):
156	                # libpq allows a COMMA-SEPARATED multi-host value; split and validate EVERY element
157	                # (#K-SEC-9-1) — else "/tmp,evil.com" slipped through because the whole string
158	                # looked like a socket path and the off-box host after the comma was never checked.
159	                for h in (p.strip() for p in val.split(",")):
160	                    if h and not is_loopback_host(h) and not is_local_socket_path(h):
161	                        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
162	                            f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); URI query "
163	                            f"{key}={h!r} points off-box"
164	                        )
165	        return uri
166	
167	    if "=" in uri:  # libpq keyword/value DSN (no URI authority)
168	        for match in _DSN_TOKEN_RE.finditer(uri):
169	            if match.group(1).strip().lower() in {"host", "hostaddr"}:
170	                raw = match.group(2).strip().strip("'\"")
171	                # Split libpq's comma-separated multi-host and validate each element (#K-SEC-9-1).
172	                for h in (p.strip() for p in raw.split(",")):
173	                    if h and not is_loopback_host(h) and not is_local_socket_path(h):
174	                        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
175	                            f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); DSN names "
176	                            f"non-loopback host {h!r}"
177	                        )
178	        return uri
179	
180	    stripped = uri.lstrip()
181	    # Windows treats ANY two leading separators (in any mix of '\' and '/') as a UNC prefix
182	    # to a remote SMB share — \\srv, //srv, /\srv, \/srv all resolve off-box (audit #D6).
183	    # (The pure '//host' form is already caught by the authority branch above; covering it
184	    # here too keeps the branch self-contained.)
185	    if len(stripped) >= 2 and stripped[0] in "\\/" and stripped[1] in "\\/":
186	        raise NonLocalEndpointError(
187	            f"{what} must be a local store (ADR-0003 data sovereignty); UNC-style path "
188	            f"{uri!r} is a remote share"
189	        )
190	    return uri  # bare path / dir store
191

8c5d7e5 fix(hardening-p9): 5 confirmed incl. security regression of pass-8 unix-socket fix
6e3d6b4 Merge pull request #169 from [REDACTED]/harden/pass-8
71ed4d5 fix(hardening-p8): CI follow-ons surfaced by actually running CI (K-CI-3 + lockfile via-comments)
harden/pass-9

content.text: Now let me find the test and run the empirical verification.

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -c "
from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri, NonLocalEndpointError
def chk(u):
    try:
        require_local_uri(u, what='x'); return 'ACCEPT'
    except NonLocalEndpointError: return 'REJECT'
print('--- should REJECT ---')
for u in ['host=/tmp,evil.com','host=/var/run/postgresql,[REDACTED]','postgresql:///db?host=/var/run/postgresql,evil.com']:
    print(chk(u), u)
print('--- should ACCEPT ---')
for u in ['host=/var/run/postgresql','host=/tmp','postgresql://localhost/db','host=127.0.0.1']:
    print(chk(u), u)
print('--- UNC should REJECT ---')
print(chk('//evil/share'), '//evil/share')
"

content.content:
--- should REJECT ---
REJECT host=/tmp,evil.com
REJECT host=/var/run/postgresql,[REDACTED]
REJECT postgresql:///db?host=/var/run/postgresql,evil.com
--- should ACCEPT ---
ACCEPT host=/var/run/postgresql
ACCEPT host=/tmp
ACCEPT postgresql://localhost/db
ACCEPT host=127.0.0.1
--- UNC should REJECT ---
REJECT //evil/share

content.text: All empirical cases pass. Now let me read the test and verify it discriminates.

content.content:
170	    # strictly MORE data-sovereign than the TCP loopback the guard already accepts. It must be
171	    # accepted, not rejected as "non-loopback".
172	    from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
173	
174	    assert require_local_uri(dsn, what="x") == dsn
175	
176	
177\[REDACTED](
178	    "dsn",
179	    [
180	        "host=/tmp,evil.com",  # multi-host: socket THEN off-box TCP
181	        "host=/var/run/postgresql,[REDACTED]",
182	        "postgresql:///db?host=/var/run/postgresql,evil.com",
183	        "postgresql:///db?host=/tmp,evil.com",
184	    ],
185	)
186	def test_require_local_uri_rejects_multihost_socket_bypass(dsn):
187	    # #K-SEC-9-1: libpq accepts a COMMA-SEPARATED multi-host value; the pass-8 socket allowance
188	    # checked the whole string, so a leading-"/" waved through an off-box host after the comma
189	    # (libpq tries the socket then falls back to the TCP host → off-box exfiltration). Every
190	    # element must be validated; any non-local element rejects.
191	    from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
192	
193	    with pytest.raises(NonLocalEndpointError):
194	        require_local_uri(dsn, what="x")
195	
196	
197\[REDACTED]("dsn", ["host=//evil/share", "host=/\\evil/share"])
198	def test_require_local_uri_still_rejects_unc_socket_lookalikes(dsn):
199	    # #K-SEC-1 guard: a UNC double-separator prefix (//server, /\server) resolves to a remote SMB
200	    # share and must still fail closed — only a SINGLE leading "/" is a local socket.
201	    from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
202	
203	    with pytest.raises(NonLocalEndpointError):
204	        require_local_uri(dsn, what="x")
205	
206	
207	def test_require_local_uri_rejects_backslash_in_authority():
208	    from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
209	
210	    with pytest.raises(NonLocalEndpointError):
211	        require_local_uri("http://evil.com\\@localhost/x", what="x")
212	
213	
214	def test_require_local_uri_fails_closed_on_unparseable():

content.text: Now let me run the test and verify it discriminates by reverting the split logic.
The test passes. Now the critical discrimination check: revert the split logic to confirm the test fails (catches the bypass).

content.text: Discrimination confirmed: under pre-fix behavior all 4 bypass DSNs are accepted, so the test would fail when reverted. Let me also verify the broader test file passes (no regressions in adjacent socket tests).

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && source .venv/bin/activate && python -m pytest -q tests/test_persistence.py -k "require_local_uri or socket or loopback" 2>&1 | tail -8

content.text:
All checks pass. Let me consider residual gaps in the fix layer.

The fix is at the right layer (`require_local_uri`), addresses root cause (multi-host split + per-element validation), and the defense is consistent across all three branches: `is_local_socket_path` rejects any comma-containing value (defense-in-depth leaf), and both the DSN keyword branch and URI query branch split on comma and validate every element with `is_loopback_host OR is_local_socket_path`. The authority branch already handled multi-host via `_authority_hosts`. Empirical results match the spec exactly, and the discrimination simulation confirms the test fails if reverted.

One minor observation (not a residual gap for this finding): the `is_local_socket_path` comma-reject is somewhat redundant now that callers split before calling it, but it is correct defense-in-depth and the docstring documents this. No security gap.

input.rationale: Fix is appropriate AND complete. Root cause was that the pass-8 leading-"/" socket allowance ran on the WHOLE host value, so libpq's comma-separated multi-host form (socket THEN off-box TCP fallback) waved through an off-box host after the comma. The fix in /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/_local.py is at the correct layer (require_local_uri, the data-sovereignty guard) and addresses the root cause directly: (1) is_local_socket_path() now returns False on any comma (defense-in-depth leaf, lines 56-58); (2) the DSN keyword/value branch splits host/hostaddr on "," and validates EVERY element with is_loopback_host OR is_local_socket_path (lines 167-178); (3) the URI query branch does the same for host/hostaddr params (lines 154-164); (4) the authority branch already handled multi-host via _authority_hosts (#D3). Empirical verification matched the spec exactly: all 3 (plus a 4th in-test) bypass DSNs REJECTED — host=/tmp,evil.com, host=/var/run/postgresql,[REDACTED], postgresql:///db?host=/var/run/postgresql,evil.com — while single socket/loopback forms (host=/var/run/postgresql, host=/tmp, postgresql://localhost/db, host=127.0.0.1) ACCEPTED and UNC //evil/share still REJECTED. Test test_require_local_uri_rejects_multihost_socket_bypass (tests/test_persistence.py:186) passes (4 params). Discrimination CONFIRMED: I re-implemented the pre-fix behavior (no comma split, whole-value socket check) and all 4 bypass DSNs were ACCEPTED, meaning the test would fail if the split is reverted. All 21 related local-guard tests pass; no regression to the legitimate single-socket allowance.

content.text: Verified K-SEC-9-1 as CONFIRMED. The fix is appropriate (correct root cause, right layer in `require_local_uri`, sound per-element validation across all three parsing branches) and complete (all spec cases pass empirically, no residual gap), and the regression test discriminates — reverting the comma-split logic causes all four bypass DSNs to be accepted, which would fail the test.
